source.title: Per-GMV Peer Comparison: PDD vs Alibaba vs JD.com (2025)

extractions.claim: Per-GMV comparison reveals PDD has the highest profit/GMV and revenue/GMV among Chinese peers
extractions.evidence:
2025 Per-GMV Metrics (estimated):

PDD Holdings:
- GMV: ~$800B (RMB 5.7T)
- Revenue: $61B (RMB 431.8B)
- Revenue/GMV (take rate): ~7.6% blended (domestic ~4.5-5%, Temu ~15-20%)
- Net Income: $14.1B (RMB 99.4B)
- Profit/GMV: ~1.8%
- Marketing spend/GMV: estimated ~2.5-3%

Alibaba (Taobao+Tmall segment):
- GMV: ~$1.1-1.2T (estimated, still #1 in China at 40-44% share)
- Taobao+Tmall segment revenue: ~$55-60B (CMR + commissions)
- Revenue/GMV: ~4.5-5.0%
- Taobao+Tmall EBITA: ~$25B
- Profit/GMV: ~2.0-2.3%
- Note: Alibaba take rate declining due to Taobao/Tmall mix shift

JD.com:
- GMV: ~$550-600B (est. from 2024 $506B + growth)
- Revenue: ~$170B (mostly 1P, gross revenue)
- Revenue/GMV: ~28-30% (misleading — JD is 1P retailer, not marketplace)
- Net Income: ~$5-6B
- Profit/GMV: ~0.9-1.0%
- Note: JD's revenue/GMV is NOT comparable to PDD/BABA due to 1P model

verification.verifier_notes: GMV figures are estimates since none of these platforms disclose GMV. Revenue/GMV for JD is deliberately excluded from comparison due to 1P/3P model difference. Key insight: PDD extracts $1.8 of profit per $100 of GMV vs Alibaba's $2.0-2.3 and JD's $0.9-1.0. PDD is closing the monetization gap with Alibaba while maintaining much faster GMV growth.

extractions.claim: PDD's blended take rate of ~7.6% is artificially high due to Temu's fully-managed model; domestic-only take rate is ~4.5-5%
extractions.evidence: PDD FY2025 revenue RMB 431.8B / estimated GMV RMB 5.7T = 7.6% blended. But this mixes two very different models: (1) Pinduoduo domestic: pure marketplace with 4.5-5% take rate (ads + commissions), (2) Temu international: fully-managed/semi-managed with 15-20% effective take rate (includes fulfillment margin). Domestic-only: OMS revenue RMB 217.7B / domestic GMV RMB 4.5-5.2T = 4.2-4.8% domestic take rate. This is converging toward Alibaba's ~4.5-5% but still slightly below.

verification.verifier_notes: The OMS-as-domestic-proxy is imperfect because some OMS revenue may come from Temu ads, and some TS revenue is domestic. But OMS is primarily domestic Pinduoduo advertising revenue.

extractions.claim: By GMV, PDD is now the #2 global e-commerce platform, within striking distance of Amazon
extractions.evidence: 2025 Global GMV rankings per ECDB: Amazon $845B, Pinduoduo $817B, Douyin/TikTok $648B. PDD's combined platform is only 3.3% behind Amazon by GMV. If Temu grows 30-40% in 2026, PDD could overtake Amazon in 2026.

verification.verifier_notes: ECDB ranking is a credible third-party source. The Amazon comparison is meaningful because both are primarily marketplace platforms (Amazon ~60% 3P). PDD overtaking Amazon in GMV in 2026 is plausible but depends on Temu's tariff resilience and domestic growth trajectory.
